source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Given are a regular hexagon with side-length 1, six squares and six equilateral triangles as shown on the right. What is the perimeter of this tessellation?
<image1>

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown on the right', so the configuration must be read from the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is primarily visual
the perimeter depends on the specific tessellation arrangement, not just the listed shapes
multi_step: solver must combine the given side length of the regular hexagon with the attached squares and equilateral triangles
finding the perimeter requires identifying which edges are internal versus exposed on the outer boundary
this is a geometry-plus-diagram counting task, involving more than direct lookup or one-step arithmetic
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='12', giving a clear target value
the result can in principle be checked by counting exposed unit edges in the depicted tessellation
asset registry passed and both image and answer are present

risk_flags: diagram-specific configuration not recoverable from text alone
verification may require accurate visual parsing of the tessellation boundary

decision_reasons: image, question, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are fully supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image dependency is acceptable here because the required diagram asset exists
verifiability is strong because the target answer is numeric and can be checked against the diagram
